title: LOGIN_ATTEMPTS HashMap never evicts entries
description: In api.rs, the LOGIN_ATTEMPTS static HashMap<String,(u32,i64)> grows unbounded. Entries are created on every login attempt from a new IP but never removed. The window_start is reset but the key persists. Over time (weeks/months of bot scanning), this causes monotonic memory growth. Unlike CIRCUIT_BREAKER which has a retain() call at >10k entries, and PASSKEY_TOKENS which cleans on every insert, LOGIN_ATTEMPTS has no eviction logic. Trigger: continuous login probes from distinct IPs (trivial for bots). Impact: slow memory leak leading to eventual OOM.
